claim: Agent build process: planning → prototype (no-code) → database setup → Python migration → UI → testing → Docker/deploy → monitoring → evals → advanced optimization.
actionable: Follow this multi-stage workflow; prototype with n8n/VoiceFlow before coding; prioritize database and monitoring early.
source_title: Revealing my COMPLETE AI Agent Blueprint

claim: Define clear V1 goals before building: what does 'done' look like? Answer prevents feature creep and enables faster iteration.
actionable: Document 3-5 core functionalities for V1; resist scope expansion until V1 is production-ready.
source_title: Revealing my COMPLETE AI Agent Blueprint

claim: No-code prototype first: use n8n, VoiceFlow, or FlowWise to build POC quickly before investing in custom code.
actionable: Spend 1-2 weeks prototyping with no-code; validate concept and tool integrations before Python migration.
source_title: Revealing my COMPLETE AI Agent Blueprint

claim: Supabase for backend: use PostgreSQL-backed database for chat history, RAG, and structured data; it's free to start and production-ready.
actionable: Set up Supabase for all agent data storage; use vector search for RAG; keep the schema simple initially.
source_title: Revealing my COMPLETE AI Agent Blueprint

claim: Python frameworks: PandticAI and LangGraph are complementary; consider AI IDEs (Windsurf, Cursor) to reduce coding burden.
actionable: Prototype agents with PandticAI; use LangGraph for complex multi-step workflows; leverage AI IDE suggestions.
source_title: Revealing my COMPLETE AI Agent Blueprint

claim: UI options: React (Bolt.DIY/Bolt.New), Streamlit (Python), or Live Agent Studio; Streamlit is easiest for MVP.
actionable: Use Streamlit for quick UIs; move to React only if you need advanced design; consider Live Agent Studio for hosted multi-agent platform.
source_title: Revealing my COMPLETE AI Agent Blueprint

claim: Testing agents with AI IDEs: Windsurf/Cursor can generate unit and integration tests; thorough testing catches edge cases and security issues.
actionable: Use AI IDE suggestions to write tests; prioritize testing for security (PII handling, injection), accuracy, and error handling.
source_title: Revealing my COMPLETE AI Agent Blueprint

claim: Docker + RunPod for GPU, DigitalOcean for CPU: RunPod has best GPU pricing; DigitalOcean best for non-GPU instances.
actionable: Containerize agents with Docker; use RunPod if you need GPU (local LLMs); use DigitalOcean for CPU-only deployments.
source_title: Revealing my COMPLETE AI Agent Blueprint

claim: API layer: always build Fast API wrapper around agent; front-end and agent are separate, communicate via API.
actionable: Create Fast API routes for agent endpoints; decouple front-end from back-end logic.
source_title: Revealing my COMPLETE AI Agent Blueprint

claim: Monitoring tools: LangSmith (LangChain), Lang Fuse (open source), LogFire (PandticAI); monitor early to catch production failures.
actionable: Set up monitoring before production deployment; choose tool matching your framework (LogFire for PandticAI, LangSmith for LangGraph).
source_title: Revealing my COMPLETE AI Agent Blueprint

claim: Advanced optimization post-launch: prompt caching, context window management, batching, load balancing, cost optimization, security, rate limiting.
actionable: Once agent is in production and stable, incrementally apply optimizations; prioritize high-impact ones (prompt caching, token batching).
source_title: Revealing my COMPLETE AI Agent Blueprint
